markets.title: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $66,000 on April 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

markets.title: Will Donald Trump post 100-119 Truth Social posts from April 3 to April 10, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Donald Trump (@realDonaldTrump), posts on Truth Social between April 3, 12:00 PM ET and April 10, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, Truth Social itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

metadata.event_title: Donald Trump # Truth Social posts April 3 - April 10, 2026?

markets.title: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $62,000 on April 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

difficulty_rationale: resolution relies on a specific numeric range (100-119) rather than a binary Yes/No, requiring precise counting
complex counting rules regarding reposts, quote posts, and the specific handling of deleted posts introduce ambiguity
dual-source resolution strategy (tracker as primary, platform as fallback) increases verification burden
need to rigorously apply exclusions (replies not on main feed) to avoid miscounting

forecast_decisions.question: Will Donald Trump post 100-119 Truth Social posts from April 3 to April 10, 2026?
forecast_decisions.rationale_summary: The decisive economic fact is that the contract resolves on XTracker and the bounded post-close evidence places the closed window at 108 counted posts, so Yes still appears underpriced at 0.81 even after discounting for modest unresolved mechanics ambiguity.

forecast_decisions.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $70,000 on April 11?
forecast_decisions.time_horizon_label: Hours remaining into the 12:00 ET / 16:00 UTC settlement minute
forecast_decisions.rationale_summary: Yes remains the likelier outcome because BTC is well above 70,000 now, but at 95.5% the market already prices that view aggressively enough that the disciplined decision is hold/watch-only rather than new exposure.

forecast_decisions.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $70,000 on April 12?
forecast_decisions.time_horizon_label: Hours remaining into the 12:00 ET / 16:00 UTC settlement minute
forecast_decisions.rationale_summary: Yes is still likelier than No because BTC remains above 70,000, but fresh Binance checks show a thinner buffer than the upstream bundle implied, making a 96% market too rich for a still-future single-minute settlement event.

forecast_decisions.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $68,000 on April 12?
forecast_decisions.time_horizon_label: Hours remaining into the April 12 12:00 ET / 16:00 UTC settlement minute
forecast_decisions.rationale_summary: BTC remains materially above 68,000 across bounded independent checks, so Yes is still likely, but the market already prices that view too aggressively for a contract settled on one future Binance minute close.

forecast_decisions.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $66,000 on April 12?
forecast_decisions.time_horizon_label: Hours remaining into the exact 12:00 ET / 16:00 UTC settlement minute
forecast_decisions.rationale_summary: BTC remains far above 66,000 across independent checks, so Yes is still the likely outcome, but the market already prices almost all of that advantage for a contract settled on one future Binance minute close.

forecast_decisions.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 12?
forecast_decisions.time_horizon_label: Final hours before the 12:00 ET / 16:00 UTC settlement minute
forecast_decisions.rationale_summary: Because the bounded inputs contain a decision-critical conflict between the assigned 0.76 market baseline and a cited live 72k price near 0.36, the disciplined output is to refuse action and demand a refreshed market update rather than bluff a false edge.

forecast_decisions.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $62,000 on April 12?
forecast_decisions.time_horizon_label: Hours remaining into the unresolved Binance BTC/USDT noon ET settlement candle on Apr 12
forecast_decisions.rationale_summary: BTC remains far above 62,000 across independent checks, so Yes is highly likely, but the market already prices that strength aggressively enough that the disciplined output is hold/watch-only rather than fresh entry.
